MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-27-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LEADERSHIP HIGH SCHOOL'S MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS IS GRANTED. THE COURT STAYS THIS ACTION TO INSURE THAT THE COURTS DO NOT MAKE CONFLICTING RULINGS ON THE "STATE ACTOR" ISSUE. THE GATEWAY ISSUE REGARDING WHETHER DEFENDANT IS OR IS NOT A STATE ACTOR SHOULD BE RESOLVED BEFORE THIS ACTION PROCEEDS ANY FURTHER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185. =(302/LCN)

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Sep-27-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Dean Royer, Siegel & Yee, 510.839.1200, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Jennifer M. Lynch, Gordon & Rees LLP, 415.986.5900, appearing for the Defendant.

Matter A R G U E D. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT LEADERSHIP HIGH SCHOOL'S Motion To Stay Proceedings is granted. The Court stays this action to insure that the courts do not make conflicting rulings on the "state actor" issue. The gateway issue regarding whether defendant is or is not a state actor should be resolved before this action proceeds any further. Order signed in open court. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185. =(302/LCN)
